title: Save changes with a pull request

blocks.content: Saving dashboard changes to a new branch lets you open a pull request for team review. This workflow enables collaboration, ensures dashboard quality through code review, and maintains approval processes before changes reach production.
To save changes with a pull request, complete the following steps:

whenTrue.content: If this guide is floating, dock it to the sidebar first.
Pop this guide out so it stays visible when the **Provisioned dashboard** save drawer opens.

blocks.content: In this section, you save changes to a new branch and open a pull request.
Make a small change to a provisioned dashboard.
Click **Save**. The **Provisioned dashboard** drawer opens on the **Details** tab.

Stay on the **Details** tab.

Optionally confirm **Repository folder** (leave empty for the repo root), **Filename**, and **Comment**.

Optionally open **Changes** to review the diff, then return to **Details**. Do that before you set **Branch** — switching tabs after setting a new branch can reset **Branch** to `main`.

On **Details**, enter a **new** **Branch** name that does not already exist, then press **Enter** (look for **Press Enter to create new branch**).

For example, enter `add-latency-metrics`.

Confirm the field shows your new branch (not `main`). Do not switch tabs after this.

Still on **Details**, with your new branch selected, click **Save**.

A successful new-branch save opens a Git Sync preview page (URL includes `/dashboard/provisioning/`).
blocks.tooltip: Creates the branch and opens the pull request preview.

blocks.content:
On the Git Sync preview page, in the success banner, click **Open pull request in GitHub**.

If you are still on the normal dashboard edit page with no banner, the save used `main` — open **Save** again and keep the new branch selected through **Save**.
blocks.tooltip: Opens GitHub with a pre-filled pull request for your dashboard changes.

blocks.content: Git Sync creates a new branch, commits your changes, and opens GitHub so you can create the pull request. The PR includes your commit message and the dashboard changes.

whenTrue.content: Dock this guide back to the sidebar to continue to the next milestone.

blocks.content: In the next milestone, you'll view and merge the pull request in GitHub.
